definition: The ROIC-WACC spread has compressed from ~8-10pp (2020-21, near-zero rates) to ~4-5pp currently. The mathematical relationship is convex: at a 10pp spread, 1pp WACC increase destroys ~10% of economic profit; at a 4pp spread, the same 1pp destroys ~25%. This explains the nonlinear increase in equity sensitivity to rate moves.
evidence: Convex mathematical relationship: rate sensitivity ~2.5x higher at current spreads vs 2020-21
Debate pair_1 confirmed the math is 'correct and underappreciated'
Single-source but follows from first principles
Mathematical identity: EVA = Invested Capital × (ROIC - WACC)
S&P 500 ROIC declined from 14-15% (2021) to 11-12% (2025E); WACC risen from 6-7% to 8-9%
In prior positive correlation episodes ROIC-WACC spreads were 8-12pp
Rate increases damage earnings through fundamental channel, not just multiple compression
open_questions: Is the spread compression uniform across sectors or concentrated in specific industries?
